1917 (2019)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $159,227,644Details
International Box Office $224,482,428
Worldwide Box Office $383,710,072
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $19,841,072
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $33,295,806
Total $53,136,878
Further financial details...

Synopsis

At the height of the First World War, two young British soldiers, Schofield and Blake are given a seemingly impossible mission. In a race against time, they must cross enemy territory and deliver a message that will stop a deadly attack on hundreds of soldiers—Blake’s own brother among them.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$576,216 (0.4% of total gross)
Legs:4.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$100,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:11 opening theaters/3,987 max. theaters, 7.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $207,450,673

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2019 (Limited) by Universal
January 10th, 2020 (Expands Wide) by Universal
International Releases: January 8th, 2020 (Wide) (Trinidad)
January 9th, 2020 (Wide) (New Zealand)
January 9th, 2020 (Wide) (Singapore)
January 9th, 2020 (Wide) (Chile)
January 10th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: March 10th, 2020 by Universal Home Entertainment
May 18th, 2020 by Universal Pictures UK
MPA Rating: R for violence, some disturbing images, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2600 (Cert #52437), 10/16/2019)
Running Time: 119 minutes
Award nomination: 2020 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Director Winner, BAFTA Best Film Winner, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Director, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner
Geographical setting: France
Plot point: Ambush, Death of a Sibling
Social setting: Behind Enemy Lines, World War I
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Time period setting: 1910s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Dreamworks LLC, Neal Street, New Republic Pictures, Reliance Entertainment, Amblin Partners
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English

Synopsis

At the height of the First World War, two young British soldiers, Schofield and Blake are given a seemingly impossible mission. In a race against time, they must cross enemy territory and deliver a message that will stop a deadly attack on hundreds of soldiers—Blake’s own brother among them.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$576,216 (0.4% of total gross)
Legs:4.30 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$100,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:11 opening theaters/3,987 max. theaters, 7.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $207,450,673

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2019 (Limited) by Universal
January 10th, 2020 (Expands Wide) by Universal
International Releases: January 8th, 2020 (Wide) (Trinidad)
January 9th, 2020 (Wide) (New Zealand)

January 9th, 2020 (Wide) (Chile)
January 10th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: March 10th, 2020 by Universal Home Entertainment
May 18th, 2020 by Universal Pictures UK
MPA Rating: R for violence, some disturbing images, and language.
(Rating bulletin 2600 (Cert #52437), 10/16/2019)
Running Time: 119 minutes
Award nomination: 2020 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Director Winner, BAFTA Best Film Winner, Critics’ Choice Winner Best Director, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Golden Globe Winner Best Picture—Drama, Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner
Geographical setting: France
Plot point: Ambush, Death of a Sibling
Social setting: Behind Enemy Lines, World War I
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Time period setting: 1910s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Dreamworks LLC, Neal Street, New Republic Pictures, Reliance Entertainment, Amblin Partners
Production Countries: United States, United Kingdom
Languages: English
